reviews.author: Ashwin Bahulkar
reviews.text: Must visit when in the area! A tad far away from the Trang an area, but absolutely worth your time and the drive. Super quiet , very few people, and friendly boat riders. The boat ride was 2 hour long and passes through the landscape, with so may varying views. Lots of bird life, and a very beautiful cave as well.

reviews.author: Kim Ng
reviews.text: Vân Long Wetland Nature Reserve was a really peaceful change of pace from the busier Ninh Bình boat routes. Tickets were 150,000 VND per person, and the boat ride lasted about 1.5 hours, gliding through calm wetlands with beautiful scenery — including a short stretch through a cave. Our rower made an effort to point out the monkeys (langurs), although we didn’t spot any on our trip. Even without wildlife sightings, the experience was worth it for the quiet, open landscape. Compared with the Tam Cốc boat ride we did the day before, Vân Long felt much more relaxed and less touristy. The boats here are bamboo (not metal), which added to the calm vibe. If you’re looking for a slower, more tranquil boat ride in Ninh Bình, this is a great option.

reviews.author: Charlie Webb
reviews.text: Beautiful landscape. 150k per person. The boat guide was very happy and friendly so we gave her a tip

reviews.author: Seunghan Yeum
reviews.text: We were two people, paid 3M for both of us. We expected 1.5-2 hours but it ended up 1 hour. The tour was very local and intimate, we were lucky to see monkey on the top of the mountain, and a lots of birds including kingfisher. Would be nice to bring binoculars. After the tours, we gave 50K as a tip. The Rower seems very disappointed. Rowers are in general old people. They will ask you to row together. It made us feel bed to not doing it. End of the tour i got completely sweated. But still enjoyed tour a lot.

reviews.author: Charles Mazalatis
reviews.text: WOW. Trang An is great but not even close in comparison to this. Trang An is Disneyland while this feels foreign, rural, and not really touristy. The landscapes were absolutely incredible. I saw endangered Delacour langurs (only 300 left in the world). It was quiet, serene, and breathtaking. Go in late afternoon. Takes about 90 min. I am glad I stayed 3 nights I Ninh Binh. Did everything at a leisurely pace and got to actually chill.

reviews.author: Linde M
reviews.text: If you want a quiet and serene boat ride, make sure to come here! It’s a beautiful and serene nature area that is quite big. We came here at midday and we only saw a handful of people and the rest of the time we were alone. The nature is gorgeous and it is so peaceful!! It is 200k per boat with a maximum of 2 people per boat.

reviews.author: Adam Drozd
reviews.text: If you are looking for a truly peaceful and authentic experience in Ninh Binh, look no further than the Van Long Nature Reserve boat tour. This trip was an absolute highlight of our journey! The Van Long wetland, often called the "Ha Long Bay on Land" offers an escape from the crowds. The entire experience was incredibly nice and calm. The silence was only broken by the gentle sound of the paddle moving through the water, allowing us to fully soak in the tranquility of this vast wetland. The views are simply outstanding. The majestic limestone karst mountains rise dramatically from the clear water, creating mirror-like reflections that are breathtakingly beautiful. Drifting through the reeds and under the cliffs felt like entering a secluded paradise. We even passed through a cave, which added a sense of adventure to the serene setting. A special mention must go to the very kind woman who drove our boat. Her friendly demeanor and careful rowing made the journey feel personal and comfortable. It's clear that the local people running these tours take pride in preserving this beautiful environment. This is a must-do trip for anyone seeking stunning natural landscapes combined with complete serenity. Highly recommended for a quiet, visually spectacular morning or afternoon!

reviews.author: Nil0
reviews.text: It's a great trip! We where all alone for the whole trip, something that really added to the tranquility of the experience. We got to see Delacour's langur up close, several unique birds and a trip inside the cave, even if it was high water. It was very hot, but we had hats and a good sunscreen which is a must, as well as water. We had our private guide that helped us in explaining what the boat rower had of knowledge. No tipping needed, but we added a bit anyway at the end. For us, this was a must do and a highlight.

reviews.author: Jen Luker
reviews.text: So peaceful and quoted, especially compared to other natural boat rides in the area. We saw lemurs. It was raining so we didn’t get to see many birds like we had hoped, but the 1000s of lotus floors in bloom made up for it.

reviews.author: Mana Jalali
reviews.text: I enjoyed every minute of the boat ride. I can't believe these guys can row a boat for 1 hour and 30 minutes straight. No breaks. While also managing to take loads of photos of me. Logic does not compute. I tried rowing for a bit and within 30 seconds my arms went up in flames, the burn was real. I asked him loads of questions in English, which he probably didn’t understand, but that’s okay, because I didn’t understand any of his answers either. It was still really nice talking to him, even if nothing made any sense. I gave him all my money. He deserves all my money. He is far superior. What a legend.

reviews.author: Erik Borkner
reviews.text: Just a wonderful one hour row boat experience. Very nice scenery, and very calm

reviews.author: C M
reviews.text: Beautiful place with beautiful scenery. The critically endangered Delacour's lutang has been a highlight of my trip to Vietnam

reviews.author: Jordon Cheung
reviews.text: This was my favorite activity of three days in Ninh Binh. I rode my motorbike there not knowing anything. I quickly realized you could not walk or motorbike through the natural reserve, you had to pay money to take a boat ride. The locals guided me to the ticket office which is approximately 200m from the boat docks. There, the ticket ladies told me I had to pay 200,000 dong because I was a single adult. I went back to board the boat and started the three hour journey around 2:40 PM. Throughout the three hours my boat lady was very kind and chill and helped me take a photo. The sun was setting as I left and it was a very nice feeling. There were not too many other tourists at this time which made it very enjoyable.

reviews.author: B M
reviews.text: Sitting in a boat in full sun exposure without any shadow cover (they should really add one), on an uncomfortable seat - a wooden plank - to see a not very varying landscape while being rowed by ladies who don’t speak any English … the pictures taken are beautiful but this felt like paying for suffering if you can’t stand direct sun burn. As concerns animals, we saw a bird, a dragonfly, some snail eggs, some cows and dead fish, nothing spectacular and none of the species in the pictures with which they attract visitors. Unless you have plenty of time, I would not recommend.

reviews.author: Bagigu Philip
reviews.text: Was a pleasant relaxing boat ride. Got to see the monkeys close up and also a few birds although it was not the right season.

reviews.author: Hélène Mousset (Lenouu7)
reviews.text: The most beautiful spot we visited during our time in the Ninh Binh region. This place is just pure nature, beautifully raw. We saw langurs, jumping from branch to branch, incredible! We hopped on a 2 seat boat for 100K per person, and the lady was very sweet. We rowed ourselves to help her a bit, but she was incredibly strong. She patiently waited until we said it was okay to go after seeing the monkeys. It's important to tip them at the end. Definitely recommend!

reviews.author: Moritz Höhne
reviews.text: What a nice boat ride. We were alone on our boat and only saw very few other boats. The views are amazing and it's not super touristy, like so many other boat rides. We spotted a lot of birds and butterflies. The short ride through the cave is pretty cool and our captain was really nice (and didn't ask for tip, we gave him one anyways). Price including parking was 100.000 vnd per person, what feels pretty fair for how long you are on the water.

reviews.author: Mary Mathews
reviews.text: Worth going to for sure! They ride you along to the beautiful mountains and caves!

reviews.author: Walter Benjamin
reviews.text: It might not be as impressive as nearby Tam Coc or Trang An, but it's a tranquil and gorgeous spot. It's not as touristy as the other places I've mentioned. A boat trip costs 150,000, and of course a tip is expected. You can go on the boat and not pass anyone. There's a small restaurant next to the ticket office where you can have a drink and something to eat. You can hire an umbrella for free. It's best to rent a motorbike or scooter and come on your own.

reviews.author: Michaela Lan Nguyenová
reviews.text: (September 2023) Someone from the restaurant near the parking area pointed us to the point where you could buy the tickets for the boat, it was a few meters away from the “pier”. Back then it cost 100k per person and at the end of the ride after cca 80 minutes, we gave our guide a tip but we absolutely weren’t pressured to. We got very lucky with the weather, we wore hats because it was extremely sunny and some ladies were selling umbrellas nearby. We didn’t get to see monkeys but the scenery was stunning nevertheless. I was mad at myself for not being able to speak Vietnamese more because our guide was lovely and tried to tell us about the formations in caves, animals and plants, I just couldn’t understand him 🥲 It’s a shame that there is a language barrier in most of the touristic places in Vietnam.

reviews.author: Herman Soh
reviews.text: The Van Long Wetland Nature Reserve boat tour was recommended to me by my local friend who was born in Ninh Binh. And boy was I glad to have taken his recommendation. Most people would have done their boat tour in Trang An, but that place is seriously touristy and overcrowded, which made Van Long all the more authentic and beautiful. When I was there it was just about half a dozen tourists, many of whom were locals, and the sights were indescribable, it was some of the most picturesque scenery I've ever seen in my life. You can see the video that I've uploaded, it doesn't look real. And in real life it's even better! I would highly recommend this place to anyone who is in Vietnam

reviews.author: Laura Baker
reviews.text: I have been travelling Vietnam for two weeks and this is the first time I had ACTUAL peace. No traffic, no kareoke, no construction, no locals screaming on their phone, no Chinese tourist buses. I only saw one other boat the whole trip. Lovely Vietnamese guide spoke 0 English but made up for it in smiles. I went at 10am on a Thursday in March. No monkeys but plenty of birds and was just lovely and zen. I did the very popular Trang a boat tour yesterday but preferred this. As I was alone I had to pay 200,000 for a boat which was a bit of a shame as it cost me double (and was arguably easier to be rowed around!) I would understand if it was busy but hey ho :) xxx

reviews.author: Theodora Mocanasu
reviews.text: We had a great time here. It is much quieter and cheaper than Tam Coc and Trang An. In order to visit the nature reserve you need to buy tickets for a boat trip. The tickets cost 200k per boat. We enjoyed 1h and a half of scenic views. We saw numerous species of birds, but unfortunately no monkeys 😢. It seems like it's a 50/50 chance you'd get to see one. We enjoyed the ride nevertheless. We gave the lady on the boat a tip because she did a great job showing us around. Unfortunately, she seemed displeased with the amount, and was not shy to show us her dissatisfaction. I am not sure if they are used to receiving way bigger tips....or why she was so upset. Either way, it was a good experience.

reviews.author: Rob “Robbydobbygoes” Adelphia
reviews.text: My experience is a bit different than the others. If you want to go straight to the boat ride, please see the other reviews. I rented a scooter for my beautiful lady and I, and this review is from that perspective. We rode in no boats, although we did see a bus load of tourists in line to do just that. We were hot with a 50 km breeze of our creating. I cannot imagine what they felt breeze-free. 103 degrees, even in the shade you will be sweating profusely. I rented the scooter in Ninh Binh and it takes about 30 minutes with no stops. We stopped in many places along the way and even a quaint village adjacent to Van Long. At Van Long, we hiked our own trail, saw beautiful birds and butterflies. We even spotted a snake slicing atop the water (it's Vietnam). If I had the choice again, I would do it the same way. The bird? A Gray-headed Swamphen, a first for me.

reviews.author: Hestiabelle Zannillestel
reviews.text: Trang A was packed like FULL so we skipped and came to Van Long. Turns out to be very delightful tranquil boat ride. 100k Dong per pax for 2 person and the boat ride is approx 1 and a half hours. Driver slowly rowing the boat and all you hear are chatters of other visitor or locals with their kids making more noise. But at some areas you will only hear the birds chirping. No chance to see any monkey as the Boatman mentioned that the weather is too cold so the monkey hide in the caves. If you wanna see monkey go Hang Múa 🤭 One really big monkey there saying HELLO 🤭🤭🤭 But a very relaxing and peaceful ride which I felt was worth it. Tip my boatman cause he used his strength to work his means and I salute that. The people worked hard so tip is a way to show our appreciation. If you like a quiet and tranquil ride, hear the birds don’t miss this place. Man made or whatever I don’t know. All is know is, Mother Nature works are always beautiful ❤️

reviews.author: Krystal Enger Roatan Real Estate
reviews.text: This is definitely the place to go for a traditional boat trip through the mountains and wetlands. Beautiful scenery, birds and the least expensive boat experience. Very easy to find, bark a bike or moto, 100,000 dong per person for over an hour ride. Not a super touristy area! The locals are so appreciative of the business and seemed very happy to show us their backyard.

reviews.author: Adventures of an Expat
reviews.text: Really enjoyed the tour. Saw a lot of wildlife including some rare monkeys! Tour guide was very nice even though she didn’t speak English at all. Would recommend if you want the experience without dealing with the tourist crowds or the tourist prices.

reviews.author: gio ses
reviews.text: Beautiful Peaceful Authentic No mass tourism...yet Compared with Trang an, they are both beautiful, but if you like silence, nature and not being harrassed, this is the choice. We were lucky we could see the monkeys jumping between trees quite well considering the distance from the boat! Only 100k x person.

reviews.author: Sarah
reviews.text: Beautiful scenery- we visited early December and were lucky to see beautiful pink lotus flowers! Pay for a boat ride (1.5 hour) to be taken around the wetlands and go into a cave. I was sceptical about doing this boat ride after visiting trang an, but it really was worth it! A lot less touristy and more peaceful- adds to the beauty of the landscape! Also visiting later in the day was well worth it as the sun starts to set and the birds start to fly back to their nests (we only saw a small flock but it was worth holding on for!)
